description: The figure presents a schematic cross-sectional diagram of a compressed Fe-Mn-Al-C steel sample illustrating the heterogeneous deformation distribution induced by friction between the contact surface and the compression platen during hot deformation. The specimen contour is shown with dashed internal boundaries demarcating three distinct zones: Zone 1 (difficult deformation zone) occupies the top and bottom central regions near the contact surfaces; Zone 2 (small deformation zone) is located at the left and right end regions; and Zone 3 (large deformation zone) comprises the central core and horizontal mid-section. A downward loading force P is indicated at the right. The caption and text state that these zones arise from uneven stress distribution during isothermal constant strain rate compression on a Gleeble-3500 simulator. This partitioning is foundational for subsequent microstructural analysis, as the degree of dynamic recrystallization and grain size evolution follows the order Zone 3 > Zone 2 > Zone 1, necessitating zone-specific metallographic observation to accurately characterize the DRX behavior across the sample section.
